symbols: demon with horns — Satan, the prince of hell, the adversary of God and humanity
flames consuming the damned — the eternal fire prepared for the devil and his angels
Latin inscription banner ('NULLA EST REDEMPTIO') — 'There is no redemption' in hell, the finality of damnation
naked suffering souls — stripped of all earthly pretense, exposed to divine judgment
description: A terrifying vertical composition showing a greenish-skinned demonic figure with horns and dark wings standing at center, arms raised in triumph. Below, naked human figures writhe in orange and red flames. A banner with Latin text ('NULLA EST REDEMPTIO') unfurls above. The demon has a grotesque face with wide eyes and a malevolent expression. A second smaller demonic figure sits atop the larger one. Rocky cave walls frame the scene. The palette is dominated by fiery oranges, reds, and the sickly green of demonic flesh.
theological_description: Memling's Hell panel, likely from a Last Judgment triptych, presents the terrible end reserved for those who reject God's mercy. The Latin inscription 'Nulla est redemptio' (there is no redemption) echoes the finality Jesus describes: 'Depart from me, you who are cursed, into the eternal fire prepared for the devil and his angels' (Matthew 25:41). The triumphant demon perversely mirrors Christ's own triumph — where Christ descends to save, Satan presides to destroy. Revelation describes this final fate: 'The devil, who deceived them, was thrown into the lake of burning sulfur... They will be tormented day and night for ever and ever' (Revelation 20:10). The rich man in Hades, separated from Lazarus by an unbridgeable chasm, cried out in torment (Luke 16:23-26) — a parable that finds its visual counterpart here. This image served as a sobering call to repentance: 'It is a dreadful thing to fall into the hands of the living God' (Hebrews 10:31).

scripture_refs.reason: Depart into eternal fire prepared for the devil and his angels — the scene depicted
The devil thrown into the lake of fire, tormented forever — the demon presiding over hell
The rich man in Hades tormented by flames — the suffering of the damned
It is a dreadful thing to fall into the hands of the living God
